headline: Prison Overcrowding at 236% With 45,000 Above Capacity
context: Malaysian prisons held 78,000 inmates against 33,000 design capacity in 2025 with 15 facilities at over 200% occupancy.

cards.big: Prisons hold 78,000 inmates at 236% capacity 45,000 above design
cards.sub: 15 facilities at over 200% occupancy creating health and safety failures

cards.big: Drug offenders comprise 42% of population with 32,760 serving sentences
cards.sub: Mandatory minimums prevent judges from using alternative sentencing

cards.big: 8 inmate deaths attributed to overcrowding health failures in 2025
cards.sub: TB infection rate in prisons 12 times higher than general population

cards.big: Rehabilitation participation dropped to 22% as facilities diverted to housing
cards.sub: Recidivism at 38% partly due to inability to provide rehabilitation

cards.big: Prison overcrowding tests whether sentencing policy accounts for system capacity
236% prison overcrowding with 45,000 above capacity. Incarceration policy must include alternatives to custody.
